title: Radian
short: The natural unit of angle: the angle that cuts off an arc equal to the radius.
full:
One radian is the angle subtended at the centre of a circle by an arc whose length equals the radius. Because a full circle has circumference 2πr, a full turn is 2π radians = 360°, so π radians = 180°.

Radians are the 'natural' angle unit in calculus: the derivative of sin x is cos x only when x is in radians. To convert, multiply degrees by π/180 to get radians, or radians by 180/π to get degrees.
